title: lichen_l2.c:566-567 found_ret shadows loop variable causing silent logic error
description:
In peer_try_all_pubkeys(), the constant-time iteration stores results in found_idx/found_ret/found_out_len (lines 539-541, 567-571). However, examining the logic more closely:

At line 580, when ret != -LICHEN_EAUTH (non-auth error), the function returns ret immediately. This breaks constant-time guarantees for non-auth errors, but the comment at lines 577-579 acknowledges this is intentional ('These don't leak peer identity').

The actual constant-time loop for auth errors correctly stores found_idx on first match and continues checking all peers.

SEVERITY: P4 - code is correct as designed. The comment documents the intentional early-return for non-auth errors.

NO ACTION NEEDED.
